nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; found in or near interstices of rock, coral, or rubble, typically at depths of 2–15 m in areas exposed to moderate surge or currents

diet.notes: In the wild, tends to feed on small crustaceans. In captivity, has been documented accepting a variety of marine meats, frozen mysis, and prepared meaty foods; live feeder shrimp may be offered to encourage feeding in newly acquired specimens.

avoidWith: small fish that fit in mouth

summary: The Two Spot Hawkfish is a small reef fish from the Indo-Pacific known for perching motionless on rockwork and ambushing small prey. Generally considered hardy and beginner-friendly, it is best kept with caution around small ornamental shrimp and tiny fish, which it may eat.
careNotes: The Two Spot Hawkfish (Amblycirrhitus bimacula) is generally considered hardy and relatively straightforward to keep. Rather than patrolling open water, it tends to perch motionless on rockwork or rubble and lunge at passing prey — a style of hunting typical of hawkfish in the family Cirrhitidae. The alternate spelling Amblycirrhites bimacula appears in some trade listings and refers to the same species. Plenty of hiding places and rockwork crevices are advised, as the species has been reported to be secretive and territorial about its preferred perch. It has been known to consume small ornamental shrimp and small fish that fit in its mouth, so it is generally regarded as reef-safe only with caution. It should not be housed with larger, more aggressive hawkfish species. Hawkfish are not considered copper-sensitive in the way that scaleless fish are, but standard caution around invertebrates applies when treating the tank.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size, temperature range, native range, taxonomy, and habitat. FishBase records adult size as 8.5 cm SL and temperature range 25.2–29.3°C (mean 28.4°C). Temperature averaged with LiveAquaria's 22.2–25.6°C to produce final range of 23.5–27.5°C.
